message:
fix(agent): race tool execution against abort signal for prompt cancellation

Previously, when the user pressed Ctrl+C during a long-running tool
execution, the abort signal was only checked between tool calls. If a
single tool hung (e.g., a shell command or network request), the agent
could not be interrupted until it timed out.

Now uses futures::select() to race the tool future against the abort
signal future. When abort fires mid-execution, the tool output is left
as None and Phase 3 records it as aborted. When no abort signal is
configured, falls back to the original sequential execution path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
